question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper mentions that blood samples were collected from 305 ART-naive persons diagnosed with HIV/AIDS in Bulgaria. These samples were used for sequence analysis to determine transmitted drug resistance and HIV-1 genetic diversity.
sentences: Blood samples were collected from 305 ART-naive persons out of 1446 patients diagnosed with HIV/AIDS in Bulgaria between 1998 and 2011 at the National HIV Reference Laboratory and/or in the clinics responsible for the management of patients with HIV in Sofia, Plovdiv and Varna; Plasma viral RNA was extracted using the MagCor Nucleic Acid Extraction Kit (RBC Bioscience, Taiwan); Protease and reverse transcriptase sequences of the HIV-1 *pol* gene were generated using the TruGene DNA Sequencing System (Siemens Healthcare, USA) following the manufacturer's protocol.

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper focuses on transmitted drug resistance mutations (TDRMs) and genetic diversity of HIV-1 in patient samples. It does not mention any in vitro drug susceptibility testing or data.
sentences: The prevalence of TDR and HIV-1 subtypes was determined in 305/1446 (21.1%) persons newly diagnosed with HIV/AIDS from 1988 to 2011; TDR mutations (TDRMs) in protease and reverse transcriptase were defined using the WHO HIV drug mutation list; Phylogenetic analysis was used to infer polymerase ( *pol* ) genotype.

question: What were the GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates?
explain: The GenBank accession numbers for the sequenced HIV isolates are explicitly listed in the "GenBank accession numbers" section of the paper.

explain: The paper consistently refers to HIV-1 throughout the text, including in the title, abstract, and results sections.
